overall_assessment: Coverage improved materially, but RR-AUTH-04 is not test-quality clean yet because the tenant-public reset success path never proves the new credential actually works, and part of the cited spillover evidence force-enables the very auth setting this slice hardens.
recommended_path: Resolve the blocking tenant reset postcondition gap and tighten the impacted-auth spillover evidence before treating RR-AUTH-04 as test-quality gate-ready.

findings.title: Tenant password-reset success coverage never proves the new password becomes usable
findings.rationale: The tenant-public reset feature test only checks for a 200 response, token deletion, and single-use rejection in PasswordRegistrationControllerTest.php:199-265. The actual mutation happens later in ProfileControllerTenant.php:92-113 and TenantProfileService.php:97-111, but no in-scope tenant test logs in with the new password or otherwise proves the stored credential changed. A regression that consumes the token and returns success without persisting the new password would still pass this packet.
findings.suggested_action: Add tenant-public evidence that a successful /auth/password_reset makes the new password usable, or directly proves the password hash changed, before closing the lane.

findings.title: The cited impacted-auth spillover suite force-enables password auth before the shared login paths run
findings.rationale: The package cites tests/Api/v1/Accounts/Auth as impacted consumer evidence, but AccountAuthFunctions.php:38-63 rewrites tenant_public_auth to ['password'] immediately before login requests, and ApiV1AccountAuthTestContract.php:18-32 seeds the same fixture in setUp. That keeps the happy-path password suite green, but it weakens its value as RR-AUTH-04 spillover evidence because the suite cannot catch accidental coupling where account/auth flows only work because the helper force-enables the exact config surface under review.
findings.suggested_action: Do not rely on the helper-driven account auth suite alone as spillover proof for RR-AUTH-04; add or isolate coverage that exercises the affected account/auth flow without mutating tenant_public_auth inside the login helper.
